mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real GDP growth indicates a supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when both CPI and FEDFUNDS rise concurrently, or the conditional Sharpe ratio of the strategy does not differ significantly from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, which systematically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: The predictive Rank IC for 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or monthly payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, triggering a risk-off market response that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when housing starts drop by more than 5 percent month-over-month, or the signal's hit rate falls below 40% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When rising unemployment coincides with declining real consumer spending, it confirms a broad-based demand shock that forces equity risk premiums higher, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sustained decline in industrial production indicates stagflationary pressures that force the Federal Reserve to prioritize price stability over market support, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when industrial production falls during high inflation periods, or the conditional predictive power vanishes when controlling for real interest rates.
